final:
I can do that, but I hit a path mismatch while checking the repo context, so I don’t want to invent the exact diff shape without reading the file.

If you want, I can still give a high-confidence proposal now from the interface you described:

- Change `PRICING` from `(input, output)` tuples to a small record, e.g. `{standard: (in, out), priority: (in, out)}` or a nested dict keyed by tier.
- Keep `PRICING[model]` backward-compatible by allowing the old tuple shape as the default/standard tier.
- Update the pricing lookup function(s) in `emptyos/capabilities/providers/openai_compat.py` that currently unpack `input, output = PRICING[model]` to read a tier-aware accessor, e.g. `get_pricing(model, tier="standard")` or equivalent.
- If billing calculations live in a separate helper, that helper should be the only caller that needs tier awareness; existing callers can continue passing just the model and get standard pricing.

Rationale: this preserves today’s tuple contract for existing code while adding a single optional dimension for priority billing, which keeps the change localized and avoids forcing every caller to learn a new schema.
